dengue.name: Dengue Fever
dengue.caused_by: Dengue virus (spread by Aedes mosquito bite)
prevention: Remove stagnant water (flower pots, tires, coolers, coconut shells)
Use mosquito repellent — Aedes bites during DAYTIME
Clean and change water in coolers weekly
dengue.treatment: No specific antiviral. Supportive care: fluids, paracetamol. AVOID aspirin/ibuprofen. Monitor platelet count. Hospitalization if warning signs.
dengue.when_to_hospital: Severe abdominal pain, persistent vomiting, bleeding, rapid drop in platelets, lethargy/restlessness.
myths: ❌ Papaya leaf juice cures dengue — NOT proven, but harmless as supplement
❌ Dengue only spreads at night — FALSE, Aedes bites in daytime
❌ You can only get dengue once — FALSE, can get it 4 times (4 serotypes)
❌ Dengue patients need complete isolation — FALSE, not person-to-person, only mosquito-to-person

malaria.name: Malaria
malaria.caused_by: Plasmodium parasite (spread by Anopheles mosquito bite)
prevention: Indoor residual spraying (IRS) in endemic areas
malaria.treatment: Anti-malarial drugs (Artemisinin-based Combination Therapy). Complete full course even if feeling better.
malaria.when_to_hospital: Severe malaria: confusion, seizures, difficulty breathing, jaundice, dark urine. Plasmodium falciparum can be fatal.
malaria.tests: Blood smear microscopy or Rapid Diagnostic Test (RDT) at PHC/district hospital.

tuberculosis.name: Tuberculosis (TB)
prevention: Close contacts of TB patients should be screened
Complete TB treatment fully — don't stop early
tuberculosis.treatment: FREE under RNTCP/NTEP. 6-month course of multiple antibiotics. Must complete full course — stopping early causes drug resistance.
tuberculosis.when_to_hospital: Cough >2 weeks with weight loss/night sweats. Get free testing at any government health facility.
tuberculosis.govt_program: National TB Elimination Programme (NTEP) — free testing and treatment at all government hospitals and PHCs. Nikshay Poshan: ₹500/month nutrition support during treatment.

diabetes.name: Diabetes (मधुमेह)
diabetes.caused_by: High blood sugar — either insufficient insulin (Type 1) or insulin resistance (Type 2)
management: Take prescribed medicines regularly — don't skip
Diet: less sugar, more fiber, balanced meals
diabetes.when_to_hospital: Blood sugar >300 mg/dL, confusion, vomiting, difficulty breathing, unconsciousness (diabetic emergency).
diabetes.free_facilities: Government PHCs provide free diabetes screening and basic medicines. Jan Aushadhi stores have affordable medicines.

hypertension.name: High Blood Pressure (उच्च रक्तचाप)
hypertension.when_to_hospital: BP >180/120 with symptoms (headache, chest pain, vision changes, difficulty breathing) — hypertensive emergency.
hypertension.complications: If untreated: stroke, heart attack, kidney damage, vision loss.

chickenpox.name: Chickenpox (चेचक)
care: Isolate from others until all blisters have crusted
Paracetamol for fever — DO NOT give aspirin
when_to_doctor: Infant, pregnant woman, or immunocompromised person affected
Blisters becoming red, warm, painful (bacterial infection)
High fever >102°F lasting more than 4 days
